key_features: Django REST API wrapper for ESPN endpoints

rate_limits.guidance: Be respectful - excessive requests may be blocked

limitations: Undocumented API - not officially supported by ESPN

repositories.citation: [2] akeaswaran. "ESPN hidden API Docs." GitHub Gist, 2024. https://gist.github.com/akeaswaran/b48b02f1c94f873c6655e7129910fc3b
key_features: Comprehensive endpoint list for multiple sports
Covers NBA, NFL, MLB, NHL, MBB, WBB, CFB
repositories.architecture: Two API domains: site.api.espn.com (scores, news) and sports.core.api.espn.com (detailed data, odds)
limitations: No odds endpoints documented in this gist
Focused on scoreboard and team data

repositories.citation: [3] ScrapeCreators. "ESPN's Hidden API: How to Access Free Sports Data." ScrapeCreators Blog, 2024. https://scrapecreators.com/blog/espn-api-free-sports-data
key_features: Explains discovery method via browser DevTools
data_quality_issues: Player IDs may reference inactive players
Player names in descriptions may not match IDs

findings: CRITICAL: /odds endpoint DOES NOT return completed games
Odds data only available for upcoming and live games
Historical movement MAY be available via /odds/{provider_id}/history/0/movement endpoint
No documented retention period for movement history
Once game completes, odds data appears to be purged
For historical backtesting, must capture odds in real-time BEFORE games complete
workarounds: Capture odds data before game starts and store locally
Use probabilities endpoint for completed games (ESPN's own projections)
Use paid services (The Odds API historical from 2020, SportsDataIO from 2019)
Scrape and cache odds from ESPN.com frontend during betting window

common_patterns: Event IDs typically match competition IDs in endpoint structure
Limit parameter commonly used (100, 200) for paginated results
Provider ID required to access specific bookmaker's odds
best_practices: Add 0.3-0.5s delay between requests to avoid rate limiting
Cache responses locally - API has no official SLA
Validate provider IDs before production use
Build fallback logic - API can change without notice
Monitor for 404/null responses indicating endpoint changes
pitfalls: Assuming odds data persists after game completion
Not validating provider ID existence before querying
Missing error handling for API changes
Relying on undocumented IDs without validation
emerging_trends: ESPN shifted from ESPN BET to DraftKings partnership (Dec 2025)
ESPN BET app shutdown may affect provider ID 41 availability
DraftKings now exclusive ESPN sportsbook (2026 rollout)
Increased scrutiny on betting APIs due to NBA scandal
More developers seeking alternatives due to instability

implementation_recommendations.scenario: Real-time odds tracking for upcoming NCAAB games
implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Use Core API /odds endpoint with polling + local caching
implementation_recommendations.rationale: API returns current odds for upcoming games reliably. Cache locally to compare opening vs current lines.
implementation_recommendations.implementation: Poll every 5-10 minutes during betting windows, store in SQLite with timestamp

implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: DO NOT rely on ESPN API - use paid service or pre-captured data
implementation_recommendations.rationale: ESPN Core API does NOT provide historical odds for completed games. Critical blocker for backtesting.
alternatives: The Odds API (historical snapshots from late 2020, $200/month)
Manual web scraping + storage (legal gray area)
Focus on CLV tracking going forward, not historical analysis

implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Query multiple provider IDs via Core API + fallback to The Odds API
implementation_recommendations.rationale: ESPN Core API supports multiple bookmakers but coverage varies. Need fallback for comprehensive line shopping.

implementation_recommendations.scenario: Building CLV tracking for paper betting
implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Capture opening odds via Core API at prediction time, capture closing odds 5 min before tip-off, calculate CLV post-game
implementation_recommendations.rationale: CLV requires both opening and closing odds. Must capture before game starts since completed games purge odds.
implementation_recommendations.critical_timing: Capture closing odds within 5-10 minutes of scheduled tip-off

popular_solutions: The Odds API - Most recommended paid alternative
Browser DevTools + network monitoring - Discovery method
controversial_topics: Legality of using undocumented ESPN APIs
Whether ESPN will shut down free access
expert_opinions: ESPN API is 'powerful but unpredictable' - can break without warning
Not suitable for production without fallbacks
Rate limiting exists but thresholds unknown
Historical odds not available - must capture in real-time
reported_issues: Odds endpoints return empty/null for completed games (EXPECTED BEHAVIOR)
Provider IDs not documented - must discover via trial
ESPN BET shutdown may affect provider availability
No authentication = no support or guarantees

blocker_issues.issue: No historical odds for completed games
blocker_issues.impact: Cannot backtest NCAAB Elo model with real ESPN odds
blocker_issues.recommended_action: Use The Odds API for historical NCAAB backtesting, or proceed with paper betting using real-time capture

blocker_issues.issue: FanDuel (58) and BetMGM (45) provider IDs unconfirmed
blocker_issues.impact: May not have access to all 5 target sportsbooks
blocker_issues.recommended_action: Test provider IDs 58 and 45 with live NCAAB games, implement fallback to The Odds API for missing books

opportunities.opportunity: Real-time odds capture for upcoming NCAAB games
opportunities.value: Enables CLV tracking for paper betting phase

opportunities.opportunity: Multi-bookmaker line shopping via ESPN Core API
opportunities.value: Find best available odds across multiple books
Identify sharp action and line steam
opportunities.implementation_effort: HIGH - endpoint behavior unclear for NCAAB

decision_points.decision: Use ESPN Core API for real-time odds or pay for The Odds API?
decision_points.recommendation: Start with ESPN Core API for paper betting phase, subscribe to The Odds API if ESPN proves unreliable
decision_points.cost_tradeoff: ESPN = free but unstable, The Odds API = $200/month with historical data and reliability
decision_points.risk: ESPN API could break during March Madness - critical failure window

decision_points.decision: Proceed with Elo backtest using simulated odds or wait for real historical data?
decision_points.recommendation: QUARANTINE current backtest results, integrate The Odds API for 2024-2025 season historical odds, re-run validation
decision_points.value: Accurate Sharpe/CLV metrics vs unrealistic simulated results

Validate DraftKings (41), Caesars (38), Bet365 (2000) provider IDs

Re-run Gatekeeper validation - expect PASS with real CLV
